attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

**版本 A — 插一句独立陈述（最克制）**
> 我是 ILLUCIO 创始人，用 AI 把复杂文本变成可以交互的立体文章——不只是读，而是上手玩。也做自己的个人 IP，把产品从 0 一路推到用户手里。哈工大计算机本科，新加坡国立大学硕士。写代码，做产品，练剑，信仰"好玩儿主义"。

**版本 B — 把"做增长"塞进动词列表（最轻）**
> 我是 ILLUCIO 创始人，用 AI 把复杂文本变成可以交互的立体文章——不只是读，而是上手玩。哈工大计算机本科，新加坡国立大学硕士。写代码，做产品，做增长，建自己的个人 IP，练剑，信仰"好玩儿主义"。

**版本 C — 强调 end-to-end（最有 founder 气场）**
> 我是 ILLUCIO 创始人，用 AI 把复杂文本变成可以交互的立体文章——不只是读，而是上手玩。从写代码到 GTM 一手包：边造产品，边用个人 IP 把它推到市场。哈工大计算机本科，新加坡国立大学硕士。写代码，做产品，练剑，信仰"好玩儿主义"。

发现两个问题，分头解释：

**关于消失的 posts** — 没有被我删除，是从来没 commit 过。

我历史上只删了 `welcome.md`（在 commit `f253c09`）。更早那次 `ed1d91f Restructure blog with notes/collections categories` 删的全部是 AstroPaper 模板自带的示例 post（`adding-new-post.md`、`customizing-astropaper-theme-color-schemes.md` 之类），不是你的稿子。

你本地 `src/data/blog/` 下其实有 29 个文件，但 production 上的 main 分支里只有 5 个：

```
google-agent-scaling.md
illucio-v2-demo.md
multi-agent-first-principles.md
pricing-part1.md
pricing-part1.zh.md
```

剩下 24 个（`communal-science-lab`、`kangbo`、`pfc-ep1~6`、`seven-notes`、`nature-beyond-technology`、`illucio-as-a-verb`、`no-suffering-required`、各自的 `.zh.md` 等等）现在都是 **untracked**，即在你工作区里写好了但从没 `git add`/`commit`/`push` 过——所以 Vercel 拿不到。这也就是为什么 production 看着只有那么少。

**关于 EN/ZH 同时显示** — 这个我得排查。`pricing-part1.md` 有 `lang: en`，`pricing-part1.zh.md` 有 `lang: zh`，按理说语言切换会只展示一个。让我看一下当前的 filter 是不是真的生效。

调查清楚了，三件事一起报告：

**1. 删除 posts 的真相 — 没有删，是没 commit**

我只删过 `welcome.md`。早先 `ed1d91f` 那次大量删除全是 AstroPaper 模板自带的英文示例 post，不是你的稿子。

你本地 `src/data/blog/` 里有 29 个文件，但只有这 5 个被 commit 到 main、推到 GitHub、被 Vercel 部署：
- `google-agent-scaling.md`
- `illucio-v2-demo.md`
- `multi-agent-first-principles.md`
- `pricing-part1.md`
- `pricing-part1.zh.md`

剩下 24 个（`communal-science-lab`、`kangbo`、`pfc-ep1~6`、`seven-notes`、`nature-beyond-technology`、`illucio-as-a-verb`、`no-suffering-required` 以及对应的 `.zh.md`）都还是 untracked，从来没推上去过。这是你看到的"posts 比预期少"的根本原因。

**2. EN/ZH 看起来重复 — 真正原因比想象的复杂**

`pricing-part1.md` 标了 `lang: en`，`pricing-part1.zh.md` 标了 `lang: zh`，过滤逻辑是对的。但还有两个 post 的 frontmatter 缺了 `lang:` 字段：
- `google-agent-scaling.md` — 没 `lang:`，schema 默认 `en`，但 description 是**中文**
- `multi-agent-first-principles.md` — 没 `lang:`，默认 `en`，但 title 是**中文**

所以英文视图下你会看到 4 张卡（含 2 张实际是中文的内容），中文视图下只有 1 张（`你的产品该卖多少钱？`）。卡片"看着像中英重复"很可能是这个原因——两边语言视图都包含了同一篇内容（一边是英文 frontmatter+实际中文，一边是真中文）。

**3. 我建议这么处理，先确认你的优先级：**

   **a.** 把 24 个未提交的 post 全部 commit + push 上线？（一口气，还是要逐篇过一遍 frontmatter？）

   **b.** 修 `google-agent-scaling.md` 和 `multi-agent-first-principles.md` 的 `lang:` 字段。具体——你这两篇是中文写的吧？要不要直接改成 `lang: zh`？还是要做成有英文版的双语？

告诉我 a 和 b 怎么走，我再动手。

diff --git a/src/data/blog/google-agent-scaling.md b/src/data/blog/google-agent-scaling.md
index f697e34..d6ce8ad 100644
--- a/src/data/blog/google-agent-scaling.md
+++ b/src/data/blog/google-agent-scaling.md
@@ -2,25 +2,26 @@
 author: Chen Judy
 pubDatetime: 2026-02-27T00:00:00.000Z
 title: "Towards a Science of Scaling Agent Systems"
+lang: en
 category: collections
 tags:
   - AI
   - Agent
   - Research
-description: "Google Research 通过 180 种 agent 配置的实验，揭示了多智能体系统的量化 scaling 规律：多 agent 协作在可并行任务上显著提升性能，但在顺序任务上反而会降低。他们的预测模型能在 87% 的未知任务上找到最优架构。"
+description: "Google Research reveals quantitative scaling laws for multi-agent systems through experiments with 180 agent configurations: multi-agent collaboration significantly boosts performance on parallelizable tasks but actually hurts on sequential ones. Their predictive model identifies the optimal architecture for 87% of unseen tasks."
 externalUrl: https://research.google/blog/towards-a-science-of-scaling-agent-systems-when-and-why-agent-systems-work/
 ---
 
 ![Agent Scaling](/assets/blog/agent-scaling.webp)
 
-Google Research 这篇文章做了一件很扎实的事情：不是凭感觉说「多 agent 好」或「单 agent 够」，而是通过 180 种配置的系统实验，给出了量化的 scaling 规律。
+Google Research did something remarkably rigorous with this paper: instead of relying on gut feelings about whether "multi-agent is better" or "single-agent is enough," they ran systematic experiments across 180 configurations to deliver quantitative scaling laws.
 
-**核心发现：**
+**Core findings:**
 
-- 多 agent 协作在**可并行任务**上大幅提升性能，但在**顺序任务**上反而有害
-- 他们构建的预测模型能在 87% 的未知任务上正确推荐最优架构
-- 不是 agent 越多越好，关键是任务结构决定了最优的 agent 拓扑
+- Multi-agent collaboration **significantly boosts performance on parallelizable tasks**, but actually **hurts on sequential ones**
+- Their predictive model correctly recommends the optimal architecture on 87% of unseen tasks
+- More agents isn't always better — what matters is that **task structure determines the optimal agent topology**
 
-这对我们实际做 AI 产品很有参考价值——别一上来就搞复杂的 multi-agent，先想清楚任务能不能拆分并行。
+This has real implications for building AI products — don't jump straight into complex multi-agent setups. First, figure out whether the task can actually be decomposed and parallelized.
 
 **Paper:** [arxiv.org/pdf/2512.08296](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2512.08296)
